channel.title: dohan.k.log
channel.description: Computer engineer who designs the 'Latest' atop the 'Legacy'

item.title: Summary of Intel x86-64 Architecture Registers
item.description:
Depending on the perspective, the number of registers in `x86-64` is described differently each time. Therefore, based on Intel’s official documentation, [`Intel® 64 and IA-32 Architectures Software Developer’s Manual Combined Volumes: 1, 2A, 2B, 2C, 2D, 3A, 3B, 3C, 3D, and 4`](https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/developer/articles/technical/intel-sdm.html), I organized the registers visible in 64-bit mode. From the full 5329-page document, most registers that appear in search results for "register" have been summarized.
## General Purpose Registers (Vol.1 p.3-13)
| Name | Description | | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | RAX | Used as an accumulator for operands and results in arithmetic operations | | RBX | Points to the address of data within a segment (Offset; related to 8086 Real Mode memory management: Segment (default DS, override possible) + Offset (BX)) | | RCX | Stores the repeat count (used by REP instructions until CX becomes 0) | | RDX | Stores I/O port numbers | | RDI | Points to data within a segment, especially used as the destination address in string operations (Segment: ES, cannot be overridden + Offset (DI)) | | RSI | Points to data within a segment, especially used as the source address in string operations (Segment: default DS, override possible + Offset (SI)) | | RBP | Points to the base of the current stack frame within the stack segment (SS) | | RSP | Points to the top of the current stack frame within the stack segment (SS) | | R8 - R15 | Additional general-purpose registers introduced in x86-64, usable for various purposes and sizes |
## Segment Registers (Vol.1 p.3-15)
| Name | Description | | ---- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | CS | Points to the current code segment (cannot be changed with MOV; only via JMP or interrupt-related instructions) | | DS | Points to the data segment (default for string operations; override possible) | | SS | Points to the stack segment | | ES | Points to the destination data segment for string operations (cannot be overridden) | | FS | Additional data segment register; usage varies depending on OS design in 64-bit mode | | GS | Additional data segment register; usage varies depending on OS design in 64-bit mode |
## FLAGS Register (Vol.1 p.3-18)
| Name | Description | | ------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | RFLAGS | Extension of EFLAGS with +32 bits in protected mode; only lower 32 bits are used (upper 32 reserved) |
## Instruction Pointer Register (Vol.1 p.3-18)
| Name | Description | | ---- | ------------------------------------------ | | RIP | Stores the address of the next instruction |
## Control Registers (Vol.3A p.2-13 / Vol.3A p.2-22)
| Name | Description | | ---- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | CR0 | Controls operating modes | | CR1 | Reserved | | CR2 | Stores the linear address that caused a page fault | | CR3 | Stores the physical address of the page directory and includes paging cache flags | | CR4 | Controls various processor-supported extensions | | CR8 | Accessible only in 64-bit mode; controls APIC TPR (Task Priority Register) | | XCR0 | Specifies memory range for state restoration during context switch (CPU restores state based on it) |
## Descriptor Table Registers (Vol.3A p.2-12)
| Name | Description | | ------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | GDTR | Holds the address of the Global Descriptor Table (defines segment properties and permissions) | | LDTR | Holds the address of the Local Descriptor Table (task-specific segment descriptors) | | IDTR | Holds the address of the Interrupt Descriptor Table (handler addresses and attributes for interrupts/exceptions) | | TR (Task Register) | Holds the address of the Task State Segment (used for transitions between user/kernel memory spaces) |
## Debug Registers (Vol.3B p.20-4 / Vol.3B p.20-42)
| Name | Description | | --------- | ------------------------------ | | DR0 - DR3 | Store breakpoint addresses | | DR4 - DR5 | Reserved | | DR6 | Indicates current debug status | | DR7 | Stores debug configuration | | DR7 | Stores debug configuration |
## MSR Registers Model-Specific Registers (Vol.4 p.2-4)
| Name | Description | | ----------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | IA32_EFER | Enables extended features: 64-bit mode activation, SYSCALL/SYSRET support, NX bit configuration | | IA32_STAR | Defines target address and segment info for SYSCALL in 32-bit compatibility mode | | IA32_LSTAR | Stores target RIP for SYSCALL in 64-bit mode | | IA32_FMASK | Specifies which RFLAGS bits are masked during system call entry | | IA32_FS_BASE | Stores FS segment base address (commonly used for thread-local storage) | | IA32_GS_BASE | Stores GS segment base address | | IA32_KERNEL_GS_BASE | Stores kernel GS base for swapping during user/kernel transitions | | IA32_SYSENTER_CS / ESP / EIP | Defines kernel CS, stack, and instruction pointer for SYSENTER/SYSEXIT in 32-bit environments | | IA32_MCU_OPT_CTRL | Controls microcode update options and mitigations | | IA32_FLUSH_CMD | Controls cache flushing (e.g., L1 cache invalidation for side-channel defense) | | IA32_TSX_CTRL | Controls Intel TSX functionality and mitigations | | IA32_MCU_ROLLBACK_MIN_ID | Stores minimum allowed microcode revision to prevent rollback attacks | | IA32_PERF_STATUS | Reports current processor performance state (P-state) | | IA32_PERF_CTL | Controls OS-requested performance state transitions | | IA32_HWP_REQUEST | Provides hints for hardware P-state control (Speed Shift) | | IA32_THERM_STATUS | Reports temperature sensor data and thermal status | | IA32_THERM_INTERRUPT | Configures thermal threshold interrupts | | IA32_OVERCLOCKING_STATUS | Indicates overclocking and undervolting protection status | | IA32_MCG_CAP | Provides Machine Check capability info | | IA32_MCG_STATUS | Indicates Machine Check status | | IA32_MCi_CTL / STATUS / ADDR / MISC | Stores hardware error information per unit | | IA32_PERFEVTSELx | Selects performance monitoring events | | IA32_PMC_FXx_CTR | Fixed-function counters (instructions, cycles, etc.) | | IA32_PMC_GPx_CTR | General-purpose performance counters | | IA32_PEBS_BASE / INDEX | Controls PEBS sampling buffer location | | IA32_X2APIC_APICID | Provides logical core APIC ID | | IA32_X2APIC_EOI | Signals end of interrupt | | IA32_X2APIC_ICR | Controls inter-processor interrupts (IPI) | | IA32_X2APIC_LVT_TIMER / LINT / THERMAL 등 | Configures local APIC interrupt vectors |
## PIC Registers (p.4)
Not included in the Intel architecture manual; referenced from legacy datasheets.
| Name | Description | | ---- | ----------------------------------- | | IRR | Records incoming interrupt requests | | ISR | Records interrupts being serviced | | IMR | Masks (blocks) selected interrupts |
## Local APIC Registers (Vol.3A p.13-6)
| Name | Description | | -------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- | | Local APIC ID Register | ID | | Local APIC Version Register | Version | | TPR | Sets minimum interrupt priority threshold | | APR | Determines core selection in multi-core arbitration | | PPR | max(TPR, ISR) | | EOI | Signals end of interrupt | | RRD | Stores remote read results | | Logical Destination Register | Specifies logical interrupt destinations | | Destination Format Register | Configures logical addressing format | | Spurious Interrupt Vector Register | Controls spurious interrupts and APIC enable | | ISR | Tracks in-service interrupts | | TMR | Indicates trigger mode (edge/level) | | IRR | Stores pending interrupts | | Error Status Register | Records APIC errors | | LVT CMCI Register | Configures corrected machine check interrupts | | ICR | Sends inter-processor interrupts | | LVT Timer Register | Configures APIC timer interrupts | | LVT Thermal Sensor Register | Configures thermal interrupts | | LVT Performance Monitoring Counters Register | Configures performance counter interrupts | | LVT LINT0 - 1 Register | Configures external interrupt pins | | LVT Error Register | Configures error interrupts | | Initial Count Register | Sets timer initial count | | Current Count Register | Holds current timer count | | Divide Configuration Register | Sets timer clock divisor |
## Floating Point / SIMD Registers
| Name | Description | | ------------ | ----------------------------------------- | | ST0 - ST7 | x87 80-bit floating-point stack registers | | Control Word | Controls FPU behavior | | Status Word | Stores FPU state and flags | | Tag Word | Indicates state of each ST register | | FIP | Last executed instruction pointer | | FDP | Last operand memory address | | FCS / FDS | Legacy segment info | | FOP | Last executed opcode | | MM0 - MM7 | 64-bit integer SIMD (reuse x87 registers) | | XMM0 - XMM15 | 128-bit SIMD (SSE) | | YMM0 - YMM15 | 256-bit SIMD (AVX) | | ZMM0 - ZMM31 | 512-bit SIMD (AVX-512) | | K0 - K7 | AVX-512 mask registers | | MXCSR | SIMD floating-point control/status |
## MSI Registers (Vol.3A p.13-35)
| Name | Description | | ------------------------ | -------------------------------------------- | | Message Address Register | Specifies APIC destination | | Message Data Register | Specifies interrupt vector and delivery mode |
## MPX Bounds Registers
| Name | Description | | ---------------------- | ---------------------------------- | | BND0 - BND3 | Define memory bounds (upper/lower) | | BNDCFGU / IA32_BNDCFGS | Configure bounds checking | | BNDSTATUS | Stores bounds violation status |
## Timer Registers
| Name | Description | | ---------------------------------- | ---------------------------------- | | TSC | 64-bit counter based on CPU cycles | | RDTSCP | Reads TSC with CPU ID atomically | | IA32_TSC_AUX | Auxiliary ID returned by RDTSCP | | Counter 0 | PIT timer (system timer) | | Counter 1 | PIT timer (DRAM refresh) | | Counter 2 | PIT timer (PC speaker) | | Control Word Register | PIT configuration | | General Capabilities and ID | HPET capabilities | | General Configuration | HPET global config | | General Interrupt Status | HPET interrupt status | | Main Counter Value | HPET main counter | | Timer N Configuration/Capabilities | HPET timer configuration | | Timer N Comparator Value | HPET comparator | | Timer N FSB Interrupt Route | Direct CPU interrupt via FSB |
## Protection-Key Rights Register (Vol.3A p.2-23)
| Name | Description | | --------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- | | PKRU | Controls user-mode memory access rights using protection keys | | IA32_PKRS | Supervisor-mode equivalent of PKRU |
